steps.title: Identify the conversion factors
steps.content: We need to convert 6.15 m/s² to miles/min². Given: 1 mile = 1,609 meters. Additionally, since 1 minute = 60 seconds, we have 1 minute² = 3,600 second².

steps.title: Multiply by conversion factors
steps.content: Multiply the acceleration by the distance and time conversion factors: $$6.15 \text{ m/s}^2 \times \frac{1 \text{ mile}}{1,609 \text{ m}} \times \frac{3,600 \text{ s}^2}{1 \text{ min}^2}$$ The first fraction converts meters to miles, and the second converts second² to minute².

steps.title: Calculate the result
steps.content: Combine the conversion factors: $$= 6.15 \times \frac{3,600}{1,609}$$ Compute the numerator: $6.15 \times 3,600 = 22,140$. Divide: $22,140 \div 1,609 = 13.759...$ Rounded to the nearest tenth: 13.8 miles per minute squared.
